name: The Foundation
description: The character is shown a vision of something they could build — a legacy, a home, a community — but the foundation requires a betrayal.
hopePole: The Foundation — build on solid ground, even if it takes a lifetime.
tollPole: The Consumption — build on blood-soaked shortcuts, consuming what others need.
setup: [PLACEHOLDER] You stand on empty ground. The soil is rich. The sky is clear. You can feel the potential — something magnificent could rise here. But the ground beneath your feet belongs to someone else.

rounds.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] A community already lives here — small, struggling, barely surviving. You could build over them, absorb them, or build alongside them. Building alongside them will take three times as long.

choices.label: Build alongside them — their home is their home
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You begin the slow work of building without displacing. The community watches with cautious hope.

choices.label: Absorb them into my vision — they'll be better off
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You fold the community into your plans. Their homes become your foundation. Efficient. Necessary. That's what you tell yourself.

choices.label: Negotiate — give them a stake in what I'm building
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You offer them a deal. Partnership, not absorption. It's slower than force but faster than charity.

rounds.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] Your creation is growing. But a rival has appeared — someone building something similar nearby. They're cutting corners, using slave labor, undercutting your work. If you don't act, their inferior version will replace yours.

choices.label: Expose their methods — let the truth destroy them
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You gather evidence and make it public. The rival's operation crumbles under scrutiny. Your creation stands on honest ground.

choices.label: Sabotage them — they don't deserve to compete
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You strike in the dark. Their foundation cracks. Their workers scatter. Your creation is the only one left standing.

choices.label: Outbuild them — let quality speak for itself
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You double down on your own work. It's exhausting, but your creation begins to outshine theirs naturally.

rounds.prompt: [PLACEHOLDER] The final vision. Your creation is complete — or nearly. But maintaining it requires a compromise that taints everything you built. Walk away clean, or stay and bend.

choices.label: I'd rather start over than build on a lie
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You tear it down to the honest foundation and begin again. It will take longer. But it will be real.

choices.label: The compromise is the cost of greatness
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You make the deal. Your creation survives — grand, imposing, and built on something rotten at its core.

choices.label: Delegate the compromise — keep my hands clean
choices.narration: [PLACEHOLDER] You find someone else to make the deal. Your creation endures. Your conscience is technically clear.

resolutions.light: [PLACEHOLDER] You emerge from the Hall with calloused hands and a clear conscience. What you built may not be the grandest, but it's honest — and it will outlast you.
resolutions.dark: [PLACEHOLDER] You emerge from the Hall with a monument at your back — grand, towering, and built on the bones of what was there before. The Dark Side Nexus admires your efficiency.
resolutions.neutral: [PLACEHOLDER] You emerge from the Hall as an architect of compromise. Your creation stands, imperfect but functional. Whether it endures depends on whether the cracks in its foundation hold.
